newsSinceJun6: CFO transition took effect June 8 (Alessandro Lagi in). Stock fell -6.5% today and roughly -20% on the week, but this is overhang from the already-announced ~$150M rights offering funding the $100M Frontier Power USA JV with Cerberus (share authorization 600M->800M approved at the June 3 annual meeting), not a new fundamental break. FY26 revenue guidance of $300-400M was reaffirmed at Q1; the AI-focused private power solution targeting up to 2 GWh over 36 months remains in play. Move judged flow/dilution-positioning driven with thesis intact.

sources.label: StockTitan - Eos stockholders approve all proposals incl. share hike to fund Frontier Power (Jun 3 annual meeting)
SEC 8-K - Eos Energy 2026 annual shareholder meeting results
StockTitan - Eos Energy and Cerberus announce Frontier Power USA funding

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already digested the adjusted-score reconciliation; since then the only window events are a pre-announced CFO effective date and continued rights-offering overhang, while the -6.5% day / -20% week sell-off with FY26 guidance reaffirmed improves asymmetry on the long-duration storage optionality thesis for a Scaling-list name at rank 27.
verdicts.bull: At -52% YTD and a $2.1B cap, a well-telegraphed ~$150M rights offering (~7% of cap) is overdiscounted; Frontier Power USA bundles financing, insurance and guarantees to make long-duration zinc storage bankable, and the 2 GWh AI private-power program gives a direct AI-buildout hook.
verdicts.neutral: Dilution is real and the offering must still price; until the record date and subscription mechanics clear, flow pressure caps the stock even if FY26 $300-400M guidance holds.
verdicts.bear: Eos has already missed its own targets once this year (February's 61% collapse), securities-fraud allegations linger, and a capital raise into a depressed price signals the balance sheet cannot self-fund the scale-up.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Rights offering pricing/closing plus first Frontier Power USA project awards or AI private-power (2 GWh) contract conversions.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Rights offering struggles or is upsized at a deeper discount, compounding dilution while litigation overhang and prior execution misses keep institutional buyers away.
